title: elymus stebbinsii subsp. septentrionalis
intro: Elymus stebbinsii subsp. septentrionalis, also known as Stebbins' wild rye, is a subspecies of perennial grass native to the western United States. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Stebbins' wild rye include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Stebbins' wild rye is often used as a tea, tincture, or infusion to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
